items.title: Element Resource Authority And Relationship-Driven Composition

requirements.statement: Ash UI shall preserve explicit framework, compilation, runtime, rendering, and governance boundaries rather than collapsing ownership into one module or one persisted document model.
Current architecture docs and governance shall treat the earlier screen-document-first direction as superseded historical context rather than as the active model.
Screen-level inline fragments may exist for glue and layout scaffolding, but they shall remain subordinate to relationship-driven element composition.
Screen compilation shall traverse the screen and element relationship graph as a first-class input rather than treating relationships as a secondary projection of a monolithic screen document.
Release and conformance automation shall report against the current .spec workspace rather than the removed specs/ matrices.
Public docs and examples shall present resource-first authoring as the default model and confine historical document-first references to explicitly approved historical materials.
The repository shall keep current architectural truth in .spec/specs/*.spec.md and durable ADRs in .spec/decisions/*.md instead of the removed top-level specs/ tree.
Repository governance validation shall use Spec Led tooling to validate the .spec workspace and write .spec/state.json.
Development and test compilation shall include the shipped basic dashboard example so example regressions are caught during ordinary local work.
The root Mix project shall publish app :ash_ui, start AshUI.Application, and expose package-level helpers through the AshUI module.
live_ui, elm_ui, and desktop_ui shall remain optional path dependencies of the root package boundary rather than mandatory runtime prerequisites.
Development and test environments shall include Spec Led tooling so the repository can validate the .spec workspace locally and in CI.
Ash UI shall expose renderer adapters and registry logic for live_ui, elm_ui, and desktop_ui without making renderer packages own authoring or compilation responsibilities.
Ash UI shall convert valid AshUI.Compilation.IUR structs into canonical unified_iur-compatible maps and validate the result before renderer consumption.
The public Elm-backed renderer naming in Ash UI shall use elm_ui, ElmUI, and the :elm renderer vocabulary rather than historical web_ui naming as the preferred current model.
Renderer packages shall remain optional path dependencies at the root package boundary so Ash UI can compile without every renderer present at runtime.
Element resources shall declare their own ui_element, ui_bindings, and ui_actions semantics through AshUI.Resource.DSL.Element.
AshUI.Resource.Authority shall persist a screen record and resource-authority payload from authored screen modules without making the persisted snapshot the primary authoring boundary.
Screen resources shall compose related element resources through Ash relationships plus ui_relationships, using inline_fragment only for limited glue.
Built-in Screen, Element, and Binding storage resources shall remain available as configurable support resources rather than as the sole public authoring API.
Runtime binding helpers shall support value reads, list reads, bidirectional updates, and action execution against Ash-side sources.
LiveView integration shall load the target screen, authorize access, compile canonical output, evaluate bindings, assign screen state, and wire update subscriptions during mount.
Runtime authorization shall gate screen mount, action execution, binding reads, and binding writes and shall return safe forbidden outcomes rather than silently proceeding.
Runtime mount, authorization, binding, and rendering flows shall emit telemetry events through the shared AshUI telemetry surface.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that tells agents to start a session with mix spec.prime before editing current truth.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents working inside the .spec workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains purpose, layout, and workflow.
When indexing and validation run, the workspace shall generate .spec/state.json containing indexed subjects, indexed decisions, and verification state.

given: a persisted screen record exists for a screen resource module
the screen composes related element resources through Ash relationships
then: the compiler traverses the resource graph
runtime state is assigned from canonical output
renderer adapters receive canonical data instead of a monolithic screen document
when: Ash UI mounts and compiles the screen

given: a developer checks out the repository
the root package dependencies are available
then: the shipped basic dashboard example is part of the compile surface
Spec Led tooling is available for workspace validation
when: the project compiles or boots in development or test

given: a dashboard screen resource uses AshUI.Resource.DSL.Screen
then: the resulting authority graph contains screen metadata, element payloads, and binding/action declarations derived from the authored resources
when: the example is persisted through AshUI.Resource.Authority

given: a current user is present in LiveView assigns
a persisted screen record exists and the actor is allowed to mount it
then: the screen is authorized, compiled, hydrated, and assigned to the socket

given: a screen contains compiled bindings and actions
then: binding helpers resolve source data, write back when allowed, and surface action results safely
when: a user changes a value or triggers an action event

meta.summary: Control-plane architecture where the resource graph is authoritative, compilation traverses relationships, and renderers consume canonical output.
Repository governance uses the .spec workspace, validates it through Spec Led tooling, and keeps public docs/examples aligned with the resource-first architecture.

The root Ash UI package publishes the ash_ui application, boots runtime services, includes the shipped example in dev/test compilation, keeps renderer dependencies optional, and carries Spec Led tooling for local governance.
Ash UI adapts internal IUR to canonical unified_iur, validates compatibility, and delegates platform output to live_ui, elm_ui, and desktop_ui adapters.
Screen and element resources own UI authoring, bindings, actions, and relationship-driven composition while shipped storage resources remain configurable support.
LiveView integration loads screens, authorizes access, compiles canonical output, evaluates bindings, routes events, and emits runtime telemetry.
Canonical workspace contract for authored specs and generated Spec Led state.

verifications.target: rg -n "relationship-driven|resource-authority|current screen/element resource graph" lib/ash_ui/resource/authority.ex lib/ash_ui/compiler.ex lib/ash_ui/liveview/liveview_integration.ex

rg -n "AshUI\.Resource\.DSL\.Screen|AshUI\.Resource\.DSL\.Element|ui_relationships|ui_bindings|ui_actions" README.md guides examples scripts/validate_authoring_governance.sh release/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md
rg -n "app: :ash_ui|mod: \\{AshUI\\.Application|def application do|def domain do|def authoring do" mix.exs lib/ash_ui.ex lib/ash_ui/application.ex
rg -n "spec_led_ex|mix spec\\.validate|mix spec\\.check|\\.spec/README\\.md" mix.exs README.md scripts/validate_specs_governance.sh .github/workflows/specs-governance.yml
rg -n "persists screen records|storage snapshot|not the primary source of truth|Screen.unified_dsl" lib/ash_ui/resource/authority.ex README.md guides/user/UG-0002-resources.md
rg -n "AshUI.Resources.Screen|AshUI.Resources.Element|AshUI.Resources.Binding|default shipped" README.md guides/user/UG-0001-getting-started.md guides/user/UG-0002-resources.md lib/ash_ui/config.ex
